mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 1 Series 135i engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned N55 engine typically costs £4,000–£6,000 supply only, with the older N54 slightly lower. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,600. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your 135i.

It depends which engine your 135i has. The N54 (2008–2010) is immensely tunable but has well‑documented HPFP, injector, wastegate and carbon buildup issues. The N55 (2010–2013) is more robust with water pump and VANOS as the main watchpoints. Check the Era Map above for your specific car.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 135i engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: N54: HPFP failure, injector failure, wastegate rattle, carbon buildup. N55: water pump failure, VANOS issues, oil filter housing gasket leaks. Both are fundamentally strong but maintenance‑intensive.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a 135i engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. An E82 135i is a £5,000–£15,000 vehicle — a £4,000–£5,000 recon is 27–100% of the car's value. For lower‑value examples (sub‑£7,000), evaluate carefully against our 70% BMW scrap threshold. A well‑maintained, lower‑mileage example justifies replacement; a tired, high‑mileage one may not.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 135i engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The 135i covers N54 (2008–2010) and N55 (2010–2013). Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm which one you have for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I prevent the HPFP failure on a 135i?

acceptedAnswer.text: On N54 cars, fitting the revised HPFP (BMW part number ending in -044) is the most effective prevention. Using high‑quality fuel and regular service can also help. If you experience long cranking, have the fuel pressure tested immediately.

problems.title: High‑Pressure Fuel Pump Failure (N54, 2008–2010)
problems.symptoms: Long crank on start, rough idle, loss of power, engine stalling, engine warning light, reduced performance under load.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (unless injectors also damaged)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms appear

problems.recommendation: The N54's HPFP is its defining weakness — a well-documented issue that plagued early cars. If you experience long cranking or loss of power, have the fuel pressure tested. BMW issued recalls for this, but many cars are now out of warranty. Replacing the HPFP with a revised unit is the permanent fix.

problems.title: Injector Failure (N54, 2008–2010)
problems.symptoms: Rough idle, misfire, fuel smell from exhaust, increased fuel consumption, engine warning light.
problems.repairCost: £600–£1,500 (injector set replacement) [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms appear

problems.recommendation: N54 injectors are another well-known weak point — they can leak or fail, causing misfires and fuel contamination. BMW issued revised injectors (index 12) which are more reliable. If you experience rough running, have the injectors tested — replacing them as a set is recommended.

problems.title: Water Pump Failure (N55, 2010–2013; also affects N54)
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss, temperature fluctuations, engine warning light, heater not working properly, overheating.
problems.repairCost: £500–£1,000 (electric water pump replacement) [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]
problems.recommendation: Both N54 and N55 use electric water pumps, which have a finite life. If you notice temperature fluctuations or coolant loss, have the cooling system inspected. Replacing the water pump preventatively at 60,000–80,000 miles is recommended by specialists.

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 135i? Check These First
buyIf: * N54 (2008–2010): HPFP replaced with revised unit (or documented recall)
* N54: injectors replaced with index 12 revised units
* Full service history with BMW-spec oil and regular intervals
avoidIf: * Long cranking on N54 — HPFP may be failing
* Rough idle on N54 — injectors may be leaking
* Temperature fluctuations — water pump may be failing
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis — potentially expensive issue

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 135i is one of BMW's most characterful performance cars — a small, rear‑wheel‑drive coupé with a legendary straight‑six. The N54 is the more tunable but less reliable engine; the N55 is the more robust choice. Both are maintenance‑intensive, so buy on documented history with key items (HPFP, injectors, water pump) replaced. A well‑maintained 135i is a future classic; a neglected one is a £4,000+ repair bill waiting to happen.

cta.label: → Compare 135i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 1 Series 135i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for smooth start; long cranking indicates HPFP issues (N54)
* Rough Idle — Check for misfire or uneven running; injector failure indicator (N54)
* Service History — including which engine era (N54 or N55) and confirmed maintenance
* Water Pump — Check for temperature fluctuations; common failure on both
* Wastegate Rattle — Listen for rattling on deceleration (N54); may indicate turbo wear
* Oil Consumption — Does it use more than 1L per 1,000 miles?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions‑related failures
